Aren't half their gachas destined to EOS?
most of them had 6+ year runs
was the greatest hit someone could have dealt to me and my friend group

but yeah they love the fact they can just poof the games and do another.
But then u actually play the game
I heard about an unironical good one of those
wasn't brown dust but YA i seen some shit
This game had such intersting gameplay
But then i actually played the game
And there's an actual large audience for this kind of stuff
I like tits as much as the next guy
But if i wanted to play a game solely for tits, there are games for that
So i never understood why there were giant badonkers/too much revealing clothes in CZN
I ended up quitting the game cuz of it's time consuming/non rewarding grind
its one of the many gachas that play you instead of playing them yourself
I honestly can't play more than 2 gachas at the same time
while you don't actually want to play them
Yeah this always drives me away
If u have a stamina system
And your farming stages don't have sweep? Your game is ass
and Im out of content once i finish latest chap
i come to play cause I enjoy
Soc is something special to me
Cuz i don't care much for the gacha part
I beat the latest ToA with 4 star units
I tried getting into its story, i'm a sucker for FFT and Tactics Ogre, but the story single player part in SoC is.. kinda boring
And i enjoy the Spiral of destiny more than i should
Im willing to say triangle strategy being my lowest rated of the single player tactics games is miles better
If you ever played triangle strategy, you'll get why i like the story
but I still rate it lower
but the other 2 are like, full of nostalgic pet peeves for me
I like triangle strategy dont get me wrong
I even bought it in VR
but i still rate it lower
Crimson story was pretty good tbh
I'll say I haven't done that one at least
Crimson is just really really good writing-wise
I hear its the one that actually got people asking for more
Top-notch storytelling and very well presented tragedy
soc's tragedy is all "eh we can loop back"
The mc doesn't exist in crimson
The story is told from young saffiyah's perspective
I'll definitely try it out when they release another single player campaign
which I dont know when will they do it
The Elaman story chapter should be upon us soon
And there's a future timeline chapter currently in the CN version
Im glad they are riding on success, its one of those games that even if I ever stop playing I don't want to ever see it die but

I has it in my earliest "games to play list"
A year before it came out

damn you really had an agenda to try it
I still fill these lists to this day

Had a whole campaign with my friends(after i convinced them)
yeah its basically divinity 2 but with a bit of a more isometric focus but same grafix
And it was the most fun I've ever had
the same way you co op on divinity 2
you'd love it if divinity was your thing

still on time if you want a very very good vibe
It's less dos2 and more bg3
it has both realtime mode and divinity turn basey' mode
But yeah I'll be getting pillars of eternity soon

embeds.title: ASTLIBRA Revision - Volcano Demon
embeds.description:
Now that's a boss! Didn't catch it's name though.

This game is very nice. One of the better JRPG's in recent years. Definitely the best 2D action RPG I've played in a very long time. 

Even though it does take notes from an IGA title, it is *NOT* a search action game. If you are interested, do not

when you master a weapon or armor or whatever
you get its ability in the menus
you can set it always for a cost
theres so many different pieces of gear
with its abilities hidden in it

but when you come back and see you were missing something so op u feel like u beat the game on a handicap
where you start as a nobody with torn clothes and then becomes basically god

Heya I’d like to ask a question as I’ve just started the game. This isn’t for help, but a question about general gameplay and endgame, since I barely know any mechanic to watch yt videos of to understand it.

The question as clear as I can make it out to be, is the gameplay control heavy and damage second, in the way that damage cannot brute force the game (regardless of difficulty) and your best way to win is to minimise damage by not getting hit?
Or does most of the game revolve around the usual damage/take damage/heal rotation that you can think of?
more to the mixture of offense and defense but you can definitely brute some boss encounters with damage and clever play (which is mostly team build oriented)
its like playing yugioh if you are familiar to the card game
you will play lots of cards to interact with different mechanics to achieve what you want to do
My second question is: does most endgame team building revolve around buffing similar types (for more damage and defense), rather than mechanically driven (where utility and control are more geared)?
have you played the game yet and scene how each realm works?
cards buffs debuffs dmg but also to interact with realm mechanics
just try it out first. its hard to explain
But I’m asking regardless is because I don’t want to see way later how endgame is like for an answer
hard to answer cause the game is not what you are expecting it to be. for example there is almost no control
yeah just try the game first or i guess have someone else answer this
It doesn’t have to be control (though I would have liked it), but it can be extremely mechanically driven (which I would really like).
Still downloading so I have time
yes its pretty mechanically driven lol
you dont hve your typical element or typing here in morimen but instead you have realms, chaos, aqueor, caro and ultra
you can run purely one realm or maximum of mixture between 2
each realm will have its own characters and very unique mechanics

To clarify:
mechanically driven not as a (this character) buffs (this character) with +15% every turn, but more
(card limit) has been reduced to 1 by a boss mechanic and you couldn’t deny it with a certain status/application or enemies revive all the time unless you kill them all at once or all allies have a stress threshold and it is near impossible to reduce it, but you can minimise how much you take.

you have dmg cards, def cards that give shields and then there are heals. there are general dmg and def up buffs debuffs then there are cards that exploits realm mechanics. then there are cards that increases play count. outside of cards, characters also have their own ultimate skill.
yeah boss mechanics are pretty deep i would say. ofc not right off the bat but you should see difficulty spike at chapter 4 last boss for example
its not straight tank and spank
That sounded pretty standard until you spoke more.
Actually have you played darkest dungeon?
skilled and careful card play is important because you dont actually get much heals and enemies hit pretty damn hard. kinda like dark souls level of hard.
Do you remember how mechanical enemies can be in the later game?
its has some darkest dungeon vibe but not entirely
no stress mechanic from stress build up for example
Nah I’m just talking about the gameplay.
boss fights i would say its similar
there is no positioning and stuff ofc
In dd bosses have weird gimmicks like not hitting the little guy in the back or stopping the torchlighter from making the cannon go boom.
So at the least I’d want mechanical gameplay like that.
the first boss makes all your cards cost more as the fight goes on
Does the turn based system have no favour who plays first? (A mechanical that determines which character/enemy act first)
in fact all story chapters has its own thing going on that influences gameplay and at later chapters outright make map progression difficult. so its not boring at least
How easy is it to access the meta and powercreep?
not really obvious i would say
if you are totally new and blind
its not obvious cause so much is good right off the bat
yeah its pretty overwhelming if you are starting blind lol
the game has multiple tool boxes
In the sense of a new character is clearly better than some old one
and alot of really unique supports
the newest character needs older ones
and those older ones are on a banner with it atm
Supports gameplay presumably revolves around this.
support can also do stuff like adding modifiers to cards that allow them to remain in your hand so that the card not get discarded end of turn
or allows card effect to activate twice
Extra action and stuff I’ve seen.
purchasable items that modify stats in dungeon
The wheels are in gacha though? I remember seeing them in videos
they add a modifier designed mostly for specific characters they correspond too
I think I got my answer to that.
Well any “item” gacha that is related to “character” in gacha I would consider as “gear” in a sense.
Are they not used by characters or the player?
you equip them to any character
if you like dd, you will probably enjoy morimen's story. it's probably the only lovecraftian horror gacha around
its not as.....dark though compared to dd but it still has its moments
nothing like pulling out wax intestines
I might pass on the story this time. I have novels and other story heavy mediums to consume
Was just wondering about the gameplay.
gameplay is heavily weighted around what your characters do and hsving a solid game plan with them
God I wish for a control/mechanically heavy turn based gacha game or something that is not entirely about damage (or at least damage can only go so far).
Sure I’ll be trying it out soon
Gore != dark. At least the good dark. The good dark is the psychological part and character development. <:Cheerup:1400771555939844149>
Game is taking way too long to download
Does the gameplay have a turn limit?
Not in endgame anymore, thank god
There are a few bosses you can outscale with defense
As long as there are bosses where characters can sustain indefinitely or work around it to win without any major damage priority.
That’s the true creative freedom of strategy gaming.
Story bosses or endgame content bosses?
I’m of the exposure that story in gacha is usually easily doable content and endgame content is doable but more painfully.
Story you wont get max rewards if you drag them out too long though
Most story stages are pretty easy yeah
End of chapter bosses can be tough though, especially the newer ones
